mechanism_narrative: A lower and declining FEDFUNDS path reduces discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so the FEDFUNDS trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling FEDFUNDS observations do not produce higher 20-day forward S&P 500 returns than rising FEDFUNDS observations in walk-forward tests with p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, labor-market slack signals weak household income growth and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE-above-6.0% observations have positive 20-day forward S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% while CPIAUCSL is rising over recent months, real policy rates are highly accommodative, supporting risk appetite and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% while CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-FEDFUNDS and rising-CPIAUCSL condition has a 60-day mean forward return that is not above the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer demand and near-term earnings support, so an always-on retail-sales trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum observations fail to outperform negative RSAFS momentum observations on 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deterioration in INDPRO signals weakening production, lower operating leverage, and declining earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls more than 1.0% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO breakdown triggers have nonnegative average 60-day forward S&P 500 returns after transaction-cost-neutral timing in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises strongly while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, housing activity transmits easier financing into construction and durable-goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST rebound and low-FEDFUNDS trigger has a 60-day hit rate below 50% across out-of-sample walk-forward periods.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with a falling UNRATE signals broad labor-income improvement rather than participation noise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if triggered PAYEMS-growth and UNRATE-decline observations do not generate a positive 20-day mean forward return in each of two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in CPIAUCSL increases expected policy tightening and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers have positive 5-day forward S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample triggered instances.
